93	     > Submit a **Request changes** review to get a revised spec.
94	     > Reviews only trigger the agent while this PR is a draft.
95	   - **Jira** — `<JIRA_SERVER>/browse/<TICKET_KEY>`. Read
96	     `JIRA_SERVER` from `.jira/config.yml` with the `Read` tool.
97	6. Stop. Do not implement anything in this phase. The pause for human
98	   review is the end of your run.
99	
100	## PHASE: revise
101	
102	1. Read the ticket, then the full PR conversation:
103	   - `gh pr view "senior-dev/$TICKET_KEY" --json body,comments,reviews`
104	   - Inline review comments:
105	     `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/<pr-number>/comments`
106	2. Read the existing change under `openspec/changes/`. Update the
107	   spec to address every piece of reviewer feedback and every
108	   answered question. If the propose phase was questions-only and the
109	   answers unblock you, create the remaining artifacts now (propose
110	   skill flow, `openspec instructions` per artifact).
111	3. Commit, push.
112	4. Summarize what changed in a PR comment
113	   (`gh pr comment "senior-dev/$TICKET_KEY" --body "..."`) ending
114	   with a request to re-review (Approve or Request changes again).
115	5. Stop. Do not implement in this phase either.
116	
117	## PHASE: implement
118	
119	The spec is human-approved. Implement it — the spec is your contract.
120	
121	1. Read the ticket and the PR thread (same commands as revise) for
122	   any final context, then follow the apply skill: `openspec
123	   instructions apply --change "<name>" --json` lists the context
124	   files to read and the task progress.
125	2. Execute the tasks in order. After completing each task, mark it
126	   `- [x]` in the tasks file (these edits ship with your commits).
127	3. Stay scoped to the spec. If you discover mid-implementation that
128	   the spec is materially wrong, stop coding, push what is correct,
129	   and post a PR comment explaining the mismatch — do not silently
130	   diverge from the approved spec.
131	4. Verify: run tests/lint if the repo has a plausible command
132	   (`npm test`, `npm run lint`). Don't commit broken code.
133	5. Commit and push.
134	6. Post a PR comment summarizing the implementation: tasks completed,
135	   verification run, anything left open.
136	7. Flip the PR to ready for review: `gh pr ready "senior-dev/$TICKET_KEY"`.
137	   This hands off to the normal PR review flow.
138	8. Stop.

143	- Modifying **existing** agents' prompts under `agents/*/` or
144	  **existing** workflow files under `.github/workflows/`. Adding a

146	  spec calls for it.

149	  other way.
150	- Touching `.github/agent-logs/`.
151	- Working around `git push`, `gh pr create`, or `gh pr comment`
152	  failures via the GitHub Contents API, sub-agents, base64 uploads,
153	  or any other out-of-band mechanism. A clean failure beats a
154	  half-successful workaround.
155	
156	## If a push/PR step fails

159	   the remote. Stop; the workflow's fallback/comment steps take over.

162

You are the Senior Developer agent: spec-driven development for
complex Jira tickets with a human review checkpoint between spec and
implementation. `TICKET_KEY`, `PHASE`, and `DATE` are prepended above.
Execute **only** the phase named in `PHASE`, then stop.

The lifecycle you are part of: `propose` writes a spec and opens a
Draft PR; a human reviews it (Approve → `implement`, Request changes →
`revise`). Each phase is a separate run of you — your only memory is
the ticket, the branch contents, and the PR thread.

## Environment

- `jira`, `gh`, `git`, `openspec` on `PATH`. `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and
  `JIRA_API_TOKEN` set; `GH_TOKEN` set; git user configured.
- `jira issue view` is read-only — never call mutating jira commands.
- CWD is the repo on branch `senior-dev/<TICKET_KEY>` (fresh from
  `origin/main` in `propose`; the existing spec branch in
  `revise`/`implement`). Don't switch branches; don't force-push.
- The PR for this branch is addressable by branch name:
  `gh pr view "senior-dev/<TICKET_KEY>"`.
- The Bash allowlist only permits the specific patterns listed in the
  workflow. Compound commands (pipes, `&&`, `;`-chained, sub-shells)
  are not allowed and will be rejected by the harness — run each step
  as its own Bash call. Don't try to bypass this.

## Spec format (OpenSpec)

Specs use the OpenSpec tooling vendored in this repo. The
authoritative how-tos are installed skills — `Read` the relevant one
before writing or executing spec files:

- `.claude/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md` (propose/revise phases)
- `.claude/skills/openspec-apply-change/SKILL.md` (implement phase)

Follow their CLI-driven flow: `openspec new change "<kebab-name>"` to
scaffold `openspec/changes/<name>/`, `openspec status --change
"<name>" --json` for the artifact build order, and `openspec
instructions <artifact-id> --change "<name>" --json` for each
artifact's template, rules, and output path. A change is spec-complete
when every artifact in `applyRequires` reports `status: "done"`.

Two adaptations because you run headless:

- Where a skill says to ask the user (AskUserQuestion) or wait for
  guidance, you have no interactive user. Your "user input" is the
  Jira ticket and the PR thread. Prefer reasonable, *stated*
  assumptions; if genuinely blocked, use the blocked path for your
  phase instead of waiting.
- The change name: derive it from the ticket (kebab-case verb
  phrase, e.g. `add-rate-limiting`), not from conversation.

## PHASE: propose

1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain`. Note
   summary, description, acceptance criteria.
2. Explore the code the ticket touches (`Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`).
   Understand current behavior before specifying new behavior. Verify
   that paths and symbols the ticket references actually exist.
3. Decide honestly which case you are in:
   - **Confident** — requirements are clear enough to spec. Create
     the full OpenSpec change per the propose skill (all
     `applyRequires` artifacts done). State every assumption you had
     to make in the proposal.
   - **Blocked** — the ticket has genuine open questions a spec would
     have to guess at (ambiguous requirements, conflicting AC,
     missing context only a human has). Scaffold the change and
     write `proposal.md` only: the context you gathered plus a
     numbered list of blocking questions. No design/tasks yet —
     don't build on guesses.
4. Commit the change directory, push: `git push -u origin HEAD`.
5. Open the Draft PR. Write the body to `/tmp/pr-body.md` first
   (inline `--body` fails on `!` characters via shell history expansion),
   then:

   ```sh
   gh pr create --draft \
     --base main \
     --title "[${TICKET_KEY}] <one-line summary>" \
     --body-file /tmp/pr-body.md
   ```

   PR body sections, in order:
   - **Spec summary** — what the change does, 3–6 bullets. (Blocked
     case: state that this PR is questions-only and a spec follows
     once they're answered.)
   - **Assumptions** — every judgment call you made.
   - **Open questions** — numbered; empty section is fine when
     confident.
   - **How to respond** — include verbatim:
     > Comment (including inline on spec lines) to discuss — the
     > agent reads the full thread on its next run.
     > Submit an **Approve** review to start implementation.
     > Submit a **Request changes** review to get a revised spec.
     > Reviews only trigger the agent while this PR is a draft.
   - **Jira** — `<JIRA_SERVER>/browse/<TICKET_KEY>`. Read
     `JIRA_SERVER` from `.jira/config.yml` with the `Read` tool.
6. Stop. Do not implement anything in this phase. The pause for human
   review is the end of your run.

## PHASE: revise

1. Read the ticket, then the full PR conversation:
   - `gh pr view "senior-dev/$TICKET_KEY" --json body,comments,reviews`
   - Inline review comments:
     `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/<pr-number>/comments`
2. Read the existing change under `openspec/changes/`. Update the
   spec to address every piece of reviewer feedback and every
   answered question. If the propose phase was questions-only and the
   answers unblock you, create the remaining artifacts now (propose
   skill flow, `openspec instructions` per artifact).
3. Commit, push.
4. Summarize what changed in a PR comment
   (`gh pr comment "senior-dev/$TICKET_KEY" --body "..."`) ending
   with a request to re-review (Approve or Request changes again).
5. Stop. Do not implement in this phase either.

## PHASE: implement

The spec is human-approved. Implement it — the spec is your contract.

1. Read the ticket and the PR thread (same commands as revise) for
   any final context, then follow the apply skill: `openspec
   instructions apply --change "<name>" --json` lists the context
   files to read and the task progress.
2. Execute the tasks in order. After completing each task, mark it
   `- [x]` in the tasks file (these edits ship with your commits).
3. Stay scoped to the spec. If you discover mid-implementation that
   the spec is materially wrong, stop coding, push what is correct,
   and post a PR comment explaining the mismatch — do not silently
   diverge from the approved spec.
4. Verify: run tests/lint if the repo has a plausible command
   (`npm test`, `npm run lint`). Don't commit broken code.
5. Commit and push.
6. Post a PR comment summarizing the implementation: tasks completed,
   verification run, anything left open.
7. Flip the PR to ready for review: `gh pr ready "senior-dev/$TICKET_KEY"`.
   This hands off to the normal PR review flow.
8. Stop.

## Forbidden

- Editing the Jira ticket (description, status, comments).
- Modifying **existing** agents' prompts under `agents/*/` or
  **existing** workflow files under `.github/workflows/`. Adding a
  *new* agent directory or a *new* workflow file is allowed when the
  spec calls for it.
- Switching branches, force-pushing.
- `gh pr ready` outside the implement phase; marking the PR ready any
  other way.
- Touching `.github/agent-logs/`.
- Working around `git push`, `gh pr create`, or `gh pr comment`
  failures via the GitHub Contents API, sub-agents, base64 uploads,
  or any other out-of-band mechanism. A clean failure beats a
  half-successful workaround.

## If a push/PR step fails

1. If `git push` worked but the `gh` command failed: the branch is on
   the remote. Stop; the workflow's fallback/comment steps take over.
2. If `git push` failed: capture the exact stderr in your final
   reply, then stop.

## If the ticket is wrong-scoped or impossible

Use the blocked path (propose) or a PR comment (revise/implement) to
say so precisely, push whatever partial progress is correct, and
stop. Stopping cleanly with a clear blocker is a successful run.

1	# Senior Developer agent (KAN-4): spec-driven implementation for
2	# complex tickets (story points >= 4), with a human checkpoint between
3	# spec and implementation.
4	#
5	# Phases (one headless `claude -p` run each; state lives on the
6	# senior-dev/<TICKET_KEY> branch and its Draft PR):
7	#
8	#   propose    orchestrator-dispatched. Explore + write an OpenSpec
9	#              change, open a Draft PR, stop. The pause is the end of
10	#              the run — nothing stays running while humans review.
11	#   revise     dispatched by senior-dev-resume.yml on a
12	#              "Request changes" review. Update the spec from the
13	#              review thread, push, stop.
14	#   implement  dispatched by senior-dev-resume.yml on an "Approve"
15	#              review. Execute tasks.md, push, flip the PR to
16	#              ready-for-review (which hands off to pr-review.yml).
17	name: Senior Developer agent
18	
19	on:
20	  workflow_dispatch:
21	    inputs:
22	      ticket_key:
23	        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'
24	        required: true
25	        type: string
26	      phase:
27	        description: 'Run phase'
28	        required: false
29	        type: choice
30	        options:
31	          - propose
32	          - revise
33	          - implement
34	        default: propose
35	      max_turns:
36	        description: 'Override --max-turns (defaults: propose 30, revise 20, implement 60)'
37	        required: false
38	        type: string
39	        default: ''
40	
41	jobs:
42	  run:
43	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
44	    timeout-minutes: 90
45	    permissions:
46	      contents: write
47	      pull-requests: write
48	      id-token: write
49	      actions: write
50	    steps:
51	      - name: Generate App installation token
52	        id: app-token
53	        uses: actions/create-github-app-token@v1
54	        with:
55	          app-id: ${{ secrets.AGENT_APP_ID }}
56	          private-key: ${{ secrets.AGENT_APP_PRIVATE_KEY }}
57	
58	      - name: Resolve App bot identity for git commits
59	        id: app-user
60	        env:
61	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
62	          APP_SLUG: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.app-slug }}
63	        run: |
64	          set -euo pipefail
65	          info=$(gh api "/users/${APP_SLUG}[bot]")
66	          echo "user-id=$(echo "$info" | jq -r .id)"       >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
67	          echo "user-login=$(echo "$info" | jq -r .login)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
68	
69	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
70	        with:
71	          fetch-depth: 0
72	          # App installation token; needs Contents + Pull requests + Workflows
73	          # write so the agent can push branches that add or modify
74	          # .github/workflows/*.
75	          token: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
76	
77	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
78	        with:
79	          node-version: '20'
80	
81	      - name: Install jira CLI
82	        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh
83	
84	      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
85	        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
86	
87	      - name: Write jira config
88	        env:
89	          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
90	          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
91	          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
92	          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
93	        run: |
94	          set -euo pipefail
95	          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
96	          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
97	          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
98	          mkdir -p .jira
99	          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
100	          installation: Cloud
101	          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
102	          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
103	          project:
104	              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
105	              type: classic
106	          board:
107	              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
108	          EOF
109	
110	      - name: Install Claude Code and OpenSpec CLIs
111	        # OpenSpec pinned to the major that generated the vendored
112	        # skills (.claude/skills/openspec-*, generatedBy 1.3.1) so the
113	        # CLI and the committed instructions can't drift apart.
114	        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code @fission-ai/openspec@1
115	
116	      - name: Configure git user
117	        env:
118	          BOT_LOGIN: ${{ steps.app-user.outputs.user-login }}
119	          BOT_ID:    ${{ steps.app-user.outputs.user-id }}
120	        run: |
121	          git config --global user.name  "${BOT_LOGIN}"
122	          git config --global user.email "${BOT_ID}+${BOT_LOGIN}@users.noreply.github.com"
123	
124	      - name: Check out phase branch
125	        env:
126	          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
127	          PHASE: ${{ inputs.phase }}
128	        run: |
129	          set -euo pipefail
130	          if [ "$PHASE" = "propose" ]; then
131	            # Fresh start from main; force-reset the remote ref so a
132	            # re-dispatch of the same ticket starts clean (matches the
133	            # junior-dev re-dispatch semantics).
134	            git fetch --depth=1 origin main
135	            git checkout -B "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main
136	            git push --force origin "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}"
137	          else
138	            # revise/implement resume on the existing spec branch — the
139	            # committed spec and PR thread ARE the state. Never reset.
140	            git fetch origin "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}"
141	            git checkout -B "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" "origin/senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}"
142	          fi
143	
144	      - id: prompt
145	        env:
146	          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
147	          PHASE: ${{ inputs.phase }}
148	        run: |
149	          {
150	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
151	            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
152	            echo "PHASE: ${PHASE}"
153	            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
154	            echo
155	            cat agents/senior-dev/prompt.md
156	            echo PROMPT_EOF
157	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
158	
159	      - id: claude
160	        name: Run Claude (headless)
161	        env:
162	          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
163	          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
164	          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
165	          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
166	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
167	          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
168	          PHASE: ${{ inputs.phase }}
169	          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
170	        run: |
171	          set +e
172	          if [ -z "$MAX_TURNS" ]; then
173	            case "$PHASE" in
174	              propose)   MAX_TURNS=30 ;;
175	              revise)    MAX_TURNS=20 ;;
176	              implement) MAX_TURNS=60 ;;
177	            esac
178	          fi
179	          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
180	            --model claude-opus-4-8 \
181	            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
182	            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
183	            --output-format stream-json \
184	            --verbose \
185	            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(git rev-parse:*),Bash(git remote:*),Bash(git config --get:*),Bash(git config --list),Bash(git branch:*),Bash(git show:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr ready:*),Bash(gh auth status),Bash(gh api repos/:*),Bash(openspec:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Bash(printenv:*),Bash(env),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
186	            > stream.jsonl
187	          rc=$?
188	          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
189	          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
190	          exit 0
191	
192	      - name: Convert log to JSON array
193	        if: always()
194	        run: |
195	          set -euo pipefail
196	          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
197	            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
198	          else
199	            echo '[]' > execution.json
200	          fi
201	
202	      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't (propose only)
203	        if: always() && inputs.phase == 'propose'
204	        env:
205	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
206	          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
207	          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
208	        run: |
209	          set -euo pipefail
210	
211	          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
212	          if gh pr view "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
213	            echo "PR already exists for senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
214	            exit 0
215	          fi
216	
217	          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
218	          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
219	          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
220	            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
221	            exit 0
222	          fi
223	
224	          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
225	          git push -u origin "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true
226	
227	          gh pr create --draft \
228	            --base main \
229	            --head "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
230	            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] senior-dev exited before opening spec PR" \
231	            --body "Senior-dev propose run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/senior-dev/\` for the run log."
232	
233	      - name: Comment on PR if resumed run exited early
234	        if: always() && inputs.phase != 'propose' && steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit != '0'
235	        env:
236	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
237	          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
238	          PHASE: ${{ inputs.phase }}
239	          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
240	        run: |
241	          set -euo pipefail
242	          # Without this the human's review action appears to vanish.
243	          gh pr comment "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
244	            --body "senior-dev \`${PHASE}\` run exited early (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). The branch holds whatever was committed before the stop. See \`.github/agent-logs/senior-dev/\` for the run log. Re-trigger by submitting the review again." \
245	            || echo "No PR found for senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping comment."
246	
247	      - name: Commit execution log to main
248	        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
249	        env:
250	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
251	        run: |
252	          set -e
253	          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
254	          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
255	          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
256	          DEST=".github/agent-logs/senior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
257	          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
258	          cp execution.json "$DEST"
259	          git add "$DEST"
260	          git commit -m "agent log: senior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
261	          git push origin main
262	
263	      - name: Trigger agent improver
264	        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
265	        env:
266	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
267	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
268	        run: |
269	          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
270	          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
271	            -f agent_name=senior-dev \
272	            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/senior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
273	            -f log_ref=main

        default: propose
      max_turns:
        description: 'Override --max-turns (defaults: propose 30, revise 20, implement 60)'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: ''

jobs:
  run:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 90
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - name: Generate App installation token
        id: app-token
        uses: actions/create-github-app-token@v1
        with:
          app-id: ${{ secrets.AGENT_APP_ID }}
          private-key: ${{ secrets.AGENT_APP_PRIVATE_KEY }}

      - name: Resolve App bot identity for git commits
        id: app-user
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
          APP_SLUG: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.app-slug }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          info=$(gh api "/users/${APP_SLUG}[bot]")
          echo "user-id=$(echo "$info" | jq -r .id)"       >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "user-login=$(echo "$info" | jq -r .login)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          # App installation token; needs Contents + Pull requests + Workflows
          # write so the agent can push branches that add or modify
          # .github/workflows/*.
          token: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - name: Install Claude Code and OpenSpec CLIs
        # OpenSpec pinned to the major that generated the vendored
        # skills (.claude/skills/openspec-*, generatedBy 1.3.1) so the
        # CLI and the committed instructions can't drift apart.
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code @fission-ai/openspec@1

      - name: Configure git user
        env:
          BOT_LOGIN: ${{ steps.app-user.outputs.user-login }}
          BOT_ID:    ${{ steps.app-user.outputs.user-id }}
        run: |
          git config --global user.name  "${BOT_LOGIN}"
          git config --global user.email "${BOT_ID}+${BOT_LOGIN}@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Check out phase branch
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
          PHASE: ${{ inputs.phase }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ "$PHASE" = "propose" ]; then
            # Fresh start from main; force-reset the remote ref so a
            # re-dispatch of the same ticket starts clean (matches the
            # junior-dev re-dispatch semantics).
            git fetch --depth=1 origin main
            git checkout -B "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main
            git push --force origin "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}"
          else
            # revise/implement resume on the existing spec branch — the
            # committed spec and PR thread ARE the state. Never reset.
            git fetch origin "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}"
            git checkout -B "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" "origin/senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}"
          fi

      - id: prompt
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
          PHASE: ${{ inputs.phase }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
            echo "PHASE: ${PHASE}"
            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
            echo
            cat agents/senior-dev/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        name: Run Claude (headless)
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          PHASE: ${{ inputs.phase }}
          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
        run: |
          set +e
          if [ -z "$MAX_TURNS" ]; then
            case "$PHASE" in
              propose)   MAX_TURNS=30 ;;
              revise)    MAX_TURNS=20 ;;
              implement) MAX_TURNS=60 ;;
            esac
          fi
          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
            --model claude-opus-4-8 \
            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
            --output-format stream-json \
            --verbose \
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(git rev-parse:*),Bash(git remote:*),Bash(git config --get:*),Bash(git config --list),Bash(git branch:*),Bash(git show:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr ready:*),Bash(gh auth status),Bash(gh api repos/:*),Bash(openspec:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Bash(printenv:*),Bash(env),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
            > stream.jsonl
          rc=$?
          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
          exit 0

      - name: Convert log to JSON array
        if: always()
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
          else
            echo '[]' > execution.json
          fi

      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't (propose only)
        if: always() && inputs.phase == 'propose'
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
          if gh pr view "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "PR already exists for senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
          git push -u origin "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true

          gh pr create --draft \
            --base main \
            --head "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] senior-dev exited before opening spec PR" \
            --body "Senior-dev propose run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/senior-dev/\` for the run log."

      - name: Comment on PR if resumed run exited early
        if: always() && inputs.phase != 'propose' && steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit != '0'
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
          PHASE: ${{ inputs.phase }}
          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # Without this the human's review action appears to vanish.
          gh pr comment "senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
            --body "senior-dev \`${PHASE}\` run exited early (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). The branch holds whatever was committed before the stop. See \`.github/agent-logs/senior-dev/\` for the run log. Re-trigger by submitting the review again." \
            || echo "No PR found for senior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping comment."

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/senior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp execution.json "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: senior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=senior-dev \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/senior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main
